text: 91. Application by purchaser to set aside sale on ground of judgment-debtor having no saleable interest. - The purchaser at any such sale in execution of a decree may apply to the Court to set aside the sale, on the ground that the judgment-debtor had no saleable interest in the property sold. Order 21, Rule 91-A High Court Amendment-[Gujarat].-In Order 21, after the existing rule 91, insert the following rule with marginal note as new rule 91-A and its marginal note: "91-A. Deposits how to be made, where execution is transferred to Collector.-When the execution of a decree has been transferred to the Collector and the sale has been conducted by the Collector or by an officer subordinate to the Collector, an application under rule 89, 90 or 91, and in the case of an application under rule 89, the deposit required by that rule, if made to the Collector, or the officer to whom the decree is referred for execution in accordance with any rule framed by the State Government under section 70 of the Code, shall be deemed to have been made to, or in the Court within, the meaning of rules 89, 90 and 91."-(17-8-1961).
